mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are positive in >65% of rate increase months.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5%, labor market slack triggers a recessionary expectation that reduces corporate profit growth forecasts, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the signal loses statistical significance (p>0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth combined with stable consumer price inflation indicates robust consumer demand without triggering aggressive monetary tightening, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to generate a positive Rank IC > 0.04 over rolling 2-year windows, or positive returns occur in <50% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production signals improving corporate capacity utilization and earnings momentum, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days exclusively during post-shock recovery phases.
failure_condition: The mechanism produces negative forward returns in >55% of observations during post-shock recovery periods, or the directional accuracy drops below 50% in this specific regime.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in housing starts reflects weakening consumer confidence and reduced construction sector activity, forecasting negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of months following a >5% housing starts decline, or the signal shows no predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) across multiple cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous nonfarm payroll growth above 200,000 and a declining unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS growth exceeds 200k while UNRATE falls
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative forward returns in >55% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above a 0.4% monthly threshold forces expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in >60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 0.4% threshold, or the signal fails to predict direction with accuracy above 50% in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth reflects expanding corporate revenue bases and improving economic fundamentals, which systematically drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >55% of quarters following positive GDPC1 revisions, or the Rank IC remains below 0.03 over a 5-year rolling evaluation window.
